content: Winclip, a vision-language grounding framework, can be adapted to use Sable's shared retention-based memory architecture for multi-agent collaborative image-text grounding, where multiple specialist Winclip agents each ground different concept domains and pull cross-domain contextual priors from the shared scalable memory. This creates a zero-shot compositional grounding system that outperforms single Winclip models on open-vocabulary grounding tasks with mixed fine-grained concepts.
source_topics: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
